from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples into a single fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    The function maps each predicate to a more readable phrase and then
    joins all clauses into one complex sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to format numbers with commas
    def fmt_number(val: str) -> str:
        try:
            # Remove possible commas and convert
            num = int(float(val.replace(",", "")))
            return f"{num:,}"
        except Exception:
            return val

    # Mapping of predicates to natural language templates.
    # {subj} and {obj} will be substituted.
    predicate_templates = {
        "country": "{subj} is based in {obj}",
        "foundingDate": "{subj} was founded on {obj}",
        "industry": "{subj} operates in the {obj}",
        "city": "{subj} is located in {obj}",
        "netIncome": "{subj} has a net income of ${obj}",
        "operatingIncome": "{subj} has an operating income of ${obj}",
        "regionServed": "{subj} serves {obj}",
        "areaTotal": "{subj} covers an area of {obj}",
        "leader": "{subj} is led by {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "{subj}'s leader holds the title of {obj}",
        "populationDensity": "{subj} has a population density of {obj} persons per km²",
        "foundationPlace": "{subj} was founded at {obj}",
        "numberOfEmployees": "{subj} employs {obj} people",
        "numberOfLocations": "{subj} operates {obj} locations",
        "service": "{subj} provides {obj}",
        "type": "{subj} is a {obj}",
        "alternativeName": "{subj} is also known as {obj}",
        "birthDate": "{subj} was born on {obj}",
        "keyPerson": "{subj} has key person {obj}",
        "cost": "{subj} cost {obj}",
        "location": "{subj} is located in {obj}",
        "product": "{subj} produces {obj}",
        "subsidiary": "{subj} owns {obj}",
        "timeZone": "{subj} lies in the {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "{subj} is part of {obj}",
        "parentCompany": "{subj} is owned by {obj}",
        "capital": "{subj}'s capital is {obj}",
        "motto": "{subj}'s motto is {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "{subj}'s official language is {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "{subj}'s leader belongs to {obj}",
        "areaUrban": "{subj} has an urban area of {obj}",
        "part": "{subj} includes {obj}",
        "revenue": "{subj} reported revenue of ${obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "{subj} includes the ethnic group {obj}",
        "governmentType": "{subj} has a government type of {obj}",
        "percentageOfAreaWater": "{subj} has {obj}% water area",
        "longName": "{subj}'s long name is {obj}",
        "populationTotal": "{subj} has a population of {obj}",
        "language": "{subj} speaks {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "{subj} sits at {obj} meters above sea level",
    }

    clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        tmpl = predicate_templates.get(t.predicate, "{subj} has {pred} {obj}")
        # format numbers where appropriate
        obj = fmt_number(t.object) if t.predicate in {
            "netIncome","operatingIncome","numberOfEmployees","numberOfLocations",
            "revenue","populationTotal","areaTotal","areaUrban","percentageOfAreaWater"
        } else t.object
        clause = tmpl.format(subj=t.subject, pred=t.predicate, obj=obj)
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Build a single sentence using commas and an 'and' before the last clause
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."

    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

Change 1: Replace:
  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """
      Convert a list of triples into a single fluent sentence.
      Each triple is expressed as “<subject> has <predicate> <object>”.
      Multiple triples are concatenated with commas and an 'and' before the last one.
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
  
      # Build simple clause for each triple
      clauses = [
          f"{t.subject} has {t.predicate} {t.object}"
          for t in triples
      ]
  
      # Join clauses into a single sentence
      if len(clauses) == 1:
          sentence = clauses[0] + "."
      else:
          sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
  
      return sentence
